title: The Arrival
timelinePosition: Directly follows Chapter 12. Opens with Sylvanas and Cyndia reaching Caer Darrow while Jaina is still in Kel'Thuzad's sanctum, and ends on the road south after the clean exit and the first Hearthglen relay.
transport: Mounted approach before open; rowboat across Darrowmere; on foot for the breach, descent, sanctum fight, and exit.
immediateObjective: Get into Scholomance, find Jaina, and get her back out before whatever runs the place closes around her.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
No correct reading of Jaina's internal fight in Chapter 12 on sight; Sylvanas only gets externals.
Keep Cyndia outside the sanctum until the exit/re-formation beats.
Keep the crack epistemic and operational rather than romantic, jealous, or melodramatic.

povKnowsAtOpen: Jaina entered the Barov estate and never came back out.
Cyndia is the only remaining wing with her, and Lyriel's absence now shapes every split decision.
The estate is occupied, heavily warded, and wrong in the way a university turned necromantic worksite would be wrong.
Jaina took the Barov trail while Sylvanas took the Arthas thread, so Sylvanas is arriving late and without Jaina's inside view.
Whatever waits below is arcane enough that Sylvanas can feel its pressure without being able to name its mechanics.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: What Jaina found in Kel'Thuzad's sanctum.
Whether Jaina is captive, compromised, or still below on purpose.
The full resonance model, the animation trigger, the Lich King chain, or the Burning Legion frame.
Whether Kel'Thuzad expects to run, fight, or simply keep talking if she reaches him.
How she will read the room once she gets there.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No correct reading of Jaina's internal fight in Chapter 12.
No betrayal language or open accusation.
No repair of the split partnership.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Kel'Thuzad is dead, Jandice is loose, and the Barov line is finished as a living Scholomance shield.
Jaina is carrying the full plague mechanism, the countermeasure frame, and the Lich King / Burning Legion chain out of Scholomance.
Sylvanas leaves with a hard, external-data-supported misread of what happened in the sanctum, and Jaina knows the misread has landed.
Cyndia rejoins them; the formation is three women now, with Lyriel's absence on the right and a new half-step crack in the middle.
Hearthglen is already overwhelmed and Arthas is moving toward Stratholme, so the road south starts immediately.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: Get inside, read the room, and pull Jaina out before the building eats the trail.
pov.surfaceObjective: Breach Caer Darrow, find Jaina, and extract her with the intelligence intact.
pov.pressureSource: She is arriving late to an arcane room with one ranger outside, one dead wing behind her, and no access to the invisible fight that may already have happened inside.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: She trusts visible behavior completely — posture, spacing, tools in hand, tone through a door — and once Jandice gives her a first read, she treats the coming tableau as something her eyes can settle cleanly.
pov.chapterTest: The sanctum gives her a perfect external picture and none of the internal context that would explain it.
pov.forcedChoice: Either refuse to trust the evidence in front of her, or trust it and carry Jaina out as a partner with a newly discovered failure mode.
pov.endStateShift: She gets Jaina out and kills Kel'Thuzad, but trust has moved into the confirmation step.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 14 opens with Sylvanas half a step ahead, closer than before, checking Jaina instead of moving level with her.

throughPov.visibleFunction: The missing partner inside the room, holding the crucial intelligence in her head and too much evidence of an unseen conversation on her hands.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Jaina is below as someone who needs extraction, not as someone who has already spent hours engaged with the man at the table.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: The chalk, the boards, the tea, the posture, and Kel'Thuzad's tone all tell Sylvanas she is looking at engagement rather than simple captivity.
throughPov.interactionRule: After the door opens, keep every exchange short. The real damage lands through silence, spacing, and what each woman now watches in the other.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Split team. Cyndia holds the outside thread while Sylvanas goes in alone to retrieve the missing half of the pair.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: They still kill like two women who know each other's hands, but after the sanctum both of them start checking before they trust.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Keep everything cold and professional. No romance bleed, no shouting, no jealous overread.
team.doNotOverstateRule: This is the first crack, not the breakup. The silence is the event.

entries.rule: Rows the crossing, holds the east-face exit, rejoins at close, carries relay traffic, and checks right twice when the formation closes again.
Encountered in the upper levels, subdued but not killed. Her wounded pride supplies the poisoned first read. She escapes before Kel'Thuzad dies.
Present only through Sylvanas's reading until the exit. She does not get to explain the sanctum here.
Calm, seated, scholarly. He dies in the sanctum and gives one last data point instead of a villain speech.
Absent. Do not drag that clue into this chapter.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 14 opens on the road south with Cyndia left, Jaina center, Sylvanas forward-right by half-step, and Jaina forced to deliver the Scholomance intelligence product without explaining the room that produced it.

factsLockedForChapter14: Jandice escaped the upper levels before Kel'Thuzad's death and remains a live loose thread.
Jaina is carrying the full resonance model, the animation trigger, the Lich King / Burning Legion chain, the countermeasure frame, and the lich-resurrection warning out of Scholomance.
Sylvanas has filed the sanctum read as a real vulnerability in Jaina and now checks where she used to trust.
The re-formed road formation is Cyndia left, Jaina center, Sylvanas forward-right by half-step.
Hearthglen is overwhelmed and Arthas is moving toward Stratholme, so the party is already heading south when Chapter 14 opens.
The old seller / repeated-farewell clue remains unresolved.
factsNotYetEarned: Any clean verbal repair of what happened in the sanctum.
Any confession from Jaina about the heartbeat she followed too far.
Any institutional acceptance of the Scholomance intelligence.
Any downgrade of the crack into simple annoyance or jealousy.

scenes.title: The Approach
scenes.sceneFunction: Get Sylvanas and Cyndia to the lake, confirm that Jaina crossed to Caer Darrow, and force a covert island breach that still leaves Sylvanas to go below alone.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina went in and did not come out; the estate is occupied and the real source lies across the lake.

enrichment.detail: Caer Darrow sits on Darrowmere Lake as an island keep above the Scholomance crypt-complex, and the lake itself is broad, still, and mist-prone enough to make the crossing feel like entering sealed ground rather than taking a simple ferry line. The island's old high elven runestone history also supports Sylvanas feeling the place as wrongly familiar even before she can parse the ward-work.

beats.goal: Reach the Darrowmere access point and confirm where Jaina actually went.
beats.conflict: The Barov estate is only the front half of the problem. Jaina's trail runs through the house and out to the lake, where the real source sits on Caer Darrow behind mist and an outer ward line.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas confirms Jaina crossed to the island, but any obvious approach over the water will be seen.

newOnPageInformation: The deepest source is on Caer Darrow, not in the manor surface alone.
Jaina reached the shore, took a boat, and left no return line behind her.
The lake crossing itself is part of the estate's security, not just the road to the island.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What is holding the island together from below.
Whether Jaina is trapped, negotiating, or already dead.
sensoryAnchors: mist sitting low over green-black water
an outer ward shimmer over the lake
rowboats half-pulled from abandoned mud
beats.transitionIntent: crossing-decision: once the shore read is clear, the team has to decide how to cross without announcing Sylvanas before she ever reaches the wall.

beats.goal: Set the crossing and preserve one surviving route back out of the island.
beats.conflict: The team is down to three women and cannot afford a loud breach; one ranger has to hold the boat and the window exit or Sylvanas loses her only clean way back out.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets a covert crossing and an east-face window breach, but taking it means leaving Cyndia on the outside thread while she goes below alone.

newOnPageInformation: The outer ward can be crossed more quietly by hugging the east wall in a low boat than by taking the public landing.
The east-face window gives Sylvanas an entry route that bypasses the front approach and the curated interior path Jaina used.
Cyndia will hold the boat, the breach point, and the relay line instead of following below.
Lyriel's absence is now built into the shape of every decision the team makes.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What waits on the island's upper levels.
Whether Sylvanas will find Jaina quickly enough for the crossing to hold.
sensoryAnchors: oarlocks wrapped in cloth
mist closing behind the boat
ward pressure softening near the east wall
Cyndia's automatic right-side check
wet stone under Sylvanas's hands
beats.transitionIntent: breach-pressure: the crossing gets Sylvanas onto the island, but she still has to get through the upper levels without using the route built for invited scholars.
beats.plantedThread: Cyndia's right-side check carries Lyriel's absence forward into the reformed three-woman formation at close.

scenes.title: The Descent
scenes.sceneFunction: Make Sylvanas earn the breach — side window, upper-service movement, Jandice ambush, then the older crypt route down to the sanctum.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Sylvanas is on the island alone and any use of the public route will light her up to the whole place.

enrichment.detail: Scholomance is canonically a necromantic academy in the crypts beneath Caer Darrow rather than a simple monster den, so service rooms, archive space, laboratories, and lecture-hall infrastructure belong beside the corpse-work. That lets the descent feel like an anti-university built out of old stone, preservative chemistry, and repurposed catacombs.

beats.goal: Get through the east-face breach, stay off the public approach, and keep Jaina's trail alive.
beats.conflict: The island route was never meant for intruders. Sylvanas has to come in through a side window, cut through service rooms and upper archives, and avoid the corridors built to funnel guests and students where the building can watch them. The off-route rooms immediately show her what the public path was designed to hide.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets inside clean, and the route immediately tells her the deeper defense is still waiting farther below.

beats.valueShift: covert entry -> the building shows its true shape
newOnPageInformation: The east-face breach leads into service, archive, and prep space above the public route, not straight into the main halls.
Scholomance's upper levels still carry the bones of the Barov estate under their necromantic conversion.
Side rooms hold peasant bodies in different stages of conversion — some still breathing, some dead and moving, some reduced to parts that still answer ward pulses.
Reanimated hands and other stripped parts are being worked as separate test stock rather than only as whole corpses.
Jaina's trail keeps going down toward older crypt architecture and never turns back.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who designed the deeper defenses.
What condition Jaina is in below the upper levels.
sensoryAnchors: lake water dripping from the breach window
blue-white fungi pulsing brighter when Sylvanas moves past
a peasant hand scratching at a table edge
dusty archive air turning to preservative cold
chemical preservation over old-rot and fresh-cut flesh
beats.transitionIntent: living-source: a living mage moving where she should not be gives Sylvanas a source instead of only architecture.

beats.goal: Neutralize the living mage in the upper levels and get something useful out of her before going lower.
beats.conflict: Jandice is angry at being dismissed, not frightened enough to stay quiet, and her jealousy makes every detail she spits out both valuable and dangerous. She also treats the rooms Sylvanas just crossed as ordinary stock space, which tells Sylvanas exactly what kind of place Scholomance really is.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas secures the source and gets real information, but the information poisons the way she will read the room below.

beats.valueShift: the building shows its true shape -> poisoned first read
newOnPageInformation: Jandice was sent away so Kel'Thuzad could keep talking to Jaina alone.
Jandice heard enough to know Jaina was asking real questions and being answered in full.
Kel'Thuzad valued the exchange enough to dismiss his own student from it.
Jandice makes plain that Jaina was given the polished route while the filthier work stayed off that tour.
The older crypt stair below the upper research levels is the fast way down if Sylvanas wants the sanctum without taking the guest route.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much of Jandice's framing is jealousy and how much is plain fact.
What the sanctum itself will show once Sylvanas reaches it.
sensoryAnchors: Jandice's reagent-burned hands under Sylvanas's grip
fungi-light catching chalk dust in a scholar's sleeve seam
anger in Jandice's voice that sounds insulted before afraid
beats.transitionIntent: poisoned-descent: Sylvanas takes the older crypt route down with Jandice's complaint already sitting in her head.
beats.disclosureProvenance: Jandice's wounded complaint is the first on-page source for Sylvanas's belief that Jaina and Kel'Thuzad were engaged as peers; it is emotionally loaded complaint, not neutral debrief.

beats.goal: Reach the sanctum without taking the public corridor and learn what kind of room waits beyond the last turn.
beats.conflict: The old crypt stair is tighter, colder, and more tightly watched than the upper levels, but it keeps Sylvanas off the route built for invited scholars. It also runs past the real waste of the institution — failed stock, butchered remains, and the parts of the work no polite tour would name. By the time she reaches the final approach, the voices inside have already confirmed Jandice's story.

outcome.summary: The catacomb route gets Sylvanas to the sanctum unseen, and the voices make the coming tableau worse before she even crosses the threshold.

beats.valueShift: poisoned first read -> certainty at the door
newOnPageInformation: The fastest covert route to the sanctum runs through older crypt architecture repurposed for lower-level research traffic.
The lower route passes discard rooms and holding spaces that make the clean sanctum above them feel like a lie told on top of the work.
Jaina and Kel'Thuzad are speaking in a real two-directional academic cadence.
The sanctum door is standing open because neither person inside was guarding against interruption.
Kel'Thuzad still sounds calm and fully in control of the room.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the room itself will show once Sylvanas enters.
Whether Jaina's face will match the voice Sylvanas is hearing.
sensoryAnchors: bone-dry dust on the older stair under ward-ripple light
a jaw still moving in a bucket of preservative
Kel'Thuzad's lecture cadence through the stone
Jaina's lower analytical register answering it
corridor cold tightening Sylvanas's bow hand
beats.transitionIntent: threshold-read: once Sylvanas steps through the door, the chapter becomes a room-read instead of an approach.

scenes.title: The Room
scenes.sceneFunction: Put Sylvanas in front of a tableau that any perfect external reader would misread the same way.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jandice's complaint and the voices through the door both say the conversation was real.
scenes.arcPressure: Everything visible in this room points one way. The only evidence against that read happened inside Jaina and is unavailable to Sylvanas.

enrichment.detail: Kel'Thuzad's canon and the Scholomance texture both support the sanctum reading as a meticulous scholar's office rather than a theatrical villain space: books, boards, specimens, and the posture of a former Kirin Tor archmage still teaching at full speed. That office-like normalcy is what makes Sylvanas's external read so convincing.

beats.goal: Cross the threshold and assess the sanctum fast enough to act.
beats.conflict: Every visible detail reinforces the same read — Kel'Thuzad seated and satisfied, Jaina near the boards instead of the exits, chalk on her hands, two different hands written across the room.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the room in one sweep, and the sweep confirms every poisoned assumption she was carrying down the corridor.

beats.valueShift: certainty at the door -> the tableau lands
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad is seated, unhurried, and not behaving like a cornered man.
Jaina is positioned in engagement posture, not defensive posture.
Jaina has chalk dust on her fingers and evidence of participation on the notation boards.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the notation in Jaina's hand is counterargument, collaboration, or both.
What happened in the hours before Sylvanas reached the room.
sensoryAnchors: chalk dust on Jaina's left hand
cold tea on a side table near Jaina
fungi-light flattening the room into a scholar's office
beats.transitionIntent: judgment-pressure: seeing the tableau is not enough; Sylvanas now has to file what it means and act on that file.

beats.dilemma: Trust the external evidence completely, or hold back from the one thing her training has always been built to do — file the room and move.
beats.decision: File Jaina as a trusted partner with a newly discovered vulnerability that now has to be watched.
beats.valueShift: the tableau lands -> the judgment locks
newOnPageInformation: The conversation lasted long enough to cover boards, burn through chalk, and cool a cup on the side table.
The evidence points to active two-way intellectual engagement, not simple coercion.
Kel'Thuzad valued the exchange enough that Jandice envied it.
After the holding rooms and discard work above, the sanctum's clean office reads as refinement rather than contradiction.
The critical missing fact lives inside Jaina, not anywhere Sylvanas can see from the doorway.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: That Jaina fought the argument for hours before the posture in the room settled where it did.
That the chalk marks in Jaina's hand were part of a counterargument rather than surrender.
Whether Jaina will be able to challenge this read without making it worse.
sensoryAnchors: the cold cup near Jaina's place at the table
two hands visible on the boards from the door
Sylvanas going still before sealing the new file
beats.transitionIntent: honest-wound: Kel'Thuzad only has to speak one honest line now to harden that judgment into damage.

beats.dilemma: Treat Kel'Thuzad's praise as manipulation and discount it, or hear the sincerity in it and accept what that sincerity does to the room.
beats.decision: Carry that judgment forward as real, finish the kill, and leave the explanation for later even though later may never come.
beats.valueShift: the judgment locks -> the crack opens
newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad openly and sincerely values Jaina's mind.
Sylvanas moves Jaina from instinctively trusted to someone she now has to watch.
Jaina sees the shift happen and knows any defense she could offer would sound like confirmation.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any later explanation can narrow the gap that just opened.
How much this new watchfulness will alter their next chapter on the road.
sensoryAnchors: Kel'Thuzad's calm voice turning a compliment into a wound
Sylvanas's total stillness rather than anger
silence settling over the sanctum harder than the lecture did
beats.transitionIntent: muscle-memory: the silence only lasts until combat habit takes over and the two women move on the same target.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Kel'Thuzad's compliment must land as genuine scholarly truth, not villain needling. The damage comes from honesty.

scenes.title: The Partnership Activates
scenes.sceneFunction: Prove the partnership still works below the level where the crack just opened, then let Kel'Thuzad die exactly as the scholar he has been all chapter.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Sylvanas has filed the misread and Jaina knows it.

enrichment.detail: Kel'Thuzad's character contract supports a death that stays calm, observational, and academically finished rather than melodramatic. Letting him die like a concluded theorem keeps the fight short and load-bearing: the real violence to the chapter is the read, not a protracted boss battle.

beats.goal: End Kel'Thuzad before he can leave the sanctum or turn the room again.
beats.conflict: The fight takes seconds, and the terrible proof is that the partnership's muscle memory still works even while its trust layer is being poisoned in real time.

outcome.summary: Kel'Thuzad is trapped and lined up for the shot, but the tiny delays inside the exchange prove the crack is already affecting how both women move.

newOnPageInformation: Jaina turns the ward layout into containment instead of trying to overpower Kel'Thuzad directly.
Sylvanas takes the northeast lane Jaina leaves open and uses it for the throat shot.
Both women confirm before they commit in ways they did not need to earlier in the partnership.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the fight's clean finish can keep this new watchfulness from following them out of the room.
What Kel'Thuzad will do with his final breath.
sensoryAnchors: ice-lattice barriers collapsing exits in white planes
bowstring note lowered by the cold
the fraction of extra space Jaina leaves in the barrier pattern
beats.transitionIntent: final-observation: the shot lands, but Kel'Thuzad still has one last line of evidence to give them before the room lets go of him.

beats.goal: Finish Kel'Thuzad and leave the sanctum with the target down.
beats.conflict: Kel'Thuzad does not die like a cornered villain. He dies like a scholar completing a thought, and the calm of that death adds one more line of evidence to Sylvanas's filed read.

outcome.summary: The target is eliminated, but his final line and his lack of fear make the room harder to forget rather than easier to leave.

newOnPageInformation: Kel'Thuzad's final spoken data point is the northeastern corridor's concentration rather than any plea or threat.
He expected death and priced it into the continuity of his work.
Sylvanas notices the calm and files it as evidence even though she does not yet have the full resurrection context Jaina has.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How useful Kel'Thuzad's last line will be once they are back on the road.
Whether Jaina can carry both the intelligence and the room's damage out without dropping one.
sensoryAnchors: the arrow in Kel'Thuzad's throat arriving like a period
Kel'Thuzad's hands staying on the table
fungi-light pulsing once and settling lower
beats.transitionIntent: exit-pressure: with the target down, the only thing left is the climb back out and the shape the formation takes once daylight finds them again.

scenes.title: The Ascent
scenes.sceneFunction: Get them out of Scholomance, re-form the three-woman line, and show the crack as spacing, timing, and silence rather than argument.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Kel'Thuzad is dead, Jaina has the intelligence, and neither woman has said the thing that matters.

enrichment.detail: Hearthglen's fortified-town status and Lordaeron's Eastweald grain-road geography support the chapter's fast southbound handoff: once the trio clears Darrowmere, they are not riding into wilderness but back into a road network already feeding a major military crisis. The relay urgency should therefore feel immediate, plausible, and spatially connected rather than conveniently timed.

beats.goal: Leave the sanctum and get back to Cyndia without losing the route or the intelligence.
beats.conflict: The exit is tactically clean, but the spacing has changed — Sylvanas runs closer, Jaina lets herself be escorted, and the whole climb turns into a field report neither woman speaks aloud.

outcome.summary: They get out clean, but the changed spacing makes the crack visible in movement.

newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas now ranges closer to Jaina than she did before the sanctum.
Jaina reads the escort spacing and does not challenge it.
Jandice is already gone from the upper levels by the time they climb out.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Sylvanas will ever share how much Jandice's complaint shaped the room-read.
Whether Jaina will try to explain the sanctum before they reach the next crisis.
sensoryAnchors: fungi-light dimmer on the way up
pressure in the walls shifting around Kel'Thuzad's absence
Jaina's silence carrying more weight than her footfalls
beats.transitionIntent: reformation: once they break back into daylight, the formation has to take Cyndia back in and carry the next crisis south at once.

beats.dilemma: Stop and force the conversation now, or keep moving because Hearthglen is already burning and the road south will not wait for private repair.
beats.decision: Keep moving. Carry the silence. Let the briefing happen before the explanation that will not come.
beats.valueShift: extraction in progress -> formation reconstituted, damaged
newOnPageInformation: Cyndia's relay says Hearthglen is overwhelmed and Arthas is already moving toward Stratholme.
The re-formed line is three women now, with Lyriel's absence on the right and Sylvanas's half-step oversight in the middle.
Jaina will give the intelligence product in Chapter 14 without giving the sanctum's internal truth.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the crack can be repaired before the next catastrophe forces them deeper together again.
Whether any institution will hear the intelligence in time to matter.
sensoryAnchors: late light on contaminated Eastweald soil
Cyndia's second right-side check when she falls back into line
Sylvanas's half-step forward changing the feel of the road
beats.transitionIntent: road-handoff: Chapter 14 picks up on this same road with Jaina briefing while they walk.

warnings: Beats 1-6 are six consecutive scene beats before the first sequel. The approach, breach, upper-level read, Jandice capture, crypt descent, and room entry need distinct prose pressure or the opening two-thirds will flatten into one continuous drive downward.

Value shifts darken in one direction across beats 3-9 — the building shows its true shape, poisoned first read, certainty at the door, the tableau lands, the judgment locks, the crack opens, functional damage. Differentiate each beat's category of loss so the middle does not become one long monotone descent.

Scene-beat outcomes are narrow: beats 1, 2, 4, 9, 10, and 11 land yes-but; beats 3, 5, and 6 land no-and; there is no clean yes. The prose will need sharp cost differentiation so success, compromise, and damage do not read as the same note.
